architecture_fit.note: Single causal softmax head with uniform prefix pattern is exactly the canonical mechanism; minimal, but construction directly builds the ideal matrix with dead QK code (dot_prod unused).

baseline_comparison.note: Relies on the framework's built-in last-token strawman (baseline_acc=0, lift=1.0); no attempt-authored ablation like no-mask or no-softmax.

faithfulness.note: Hand-built synthetic head, no model and no ablation; README admits hand-built but never proposes the causal check the rubric asks of synthetic attempts.

operating_range.note: Holds perfectly (accuracy=1.0, leakage=0) across the full 8->128 sweep; exact by construction so no degradation shown at any edge.

hardcoded_weights_bonus.note: Entire mechanism is a hand-set circuit reproducing the ideal uniform-causal pattern exactly across all lengths.

visual_judgement.note: app.py fails boot-check: imports nonexistent load_results, references undefined __dirname, and uses unimported Dict/Tuple annotations; demo also plots a placeholder ideal matrix, not the real head.

visualisation_rationale.note: README justifies the lower-triangle uniform heatmap and robustness=1.0 line as the legibility check, reasonable but generic and undermined by the non-functional app.

notes: The hand-built single-head construction is the canonical prefix-sum mechanism and scores perfectly on every automated metric across the full sweep, earning the hardcoded-weights bonus. It is held back by a weak causal narrative (no ablation or proposed faithfulness check), reliance on the framework's built-in baseline rather than an authored strawman, and an app.py that fails the boot-check on multiple import/name errors. A correct core result whose evidence and visualisation are not yet trustworthy.
